title: Close #1079 runtime reduction child slices

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Continue the #1079 runtime-reduction lane until its open child slices are honestly implemented or routed.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Reduce shipped/runtime Python coupling without adding new user workflow surfaces; keep command-generation as maintainer tooling and preserve generated CLI behavior.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Choose the smallest child-slice order, use declarative contracts/primitives where already present, and close children only when proof covers their acceptance signals.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.
canonical_core.next_action: Finish the #1086 shipped-runtime dependency slice, then continue through #1082, #1083, #1084, and #1085 as bounded commits; reconcile #1080 and #1081 if current archive/proof already closes them.
completion_criteria: #1079 child issues #1080-#1086 are either closed by concrete proof in this branch or explicitly left open with a named continuation owner and reason.
Shipped agentic-workspace runtime no longer depends on command-generation for normal installed CLI execution.
Accepted-runtime/codegen checks report or prove the runtime-reduction signals needed by the implemented child slices.
canonical_core.continuation_owner: #1079 if any child slice remains open after proof

goal: Create a bounded plan for Close #1079 runtime reduction child slices.
non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: #1079 reduces accepted package-domain Python after Python completion while keeping the #892 codegen epic open for future non-Python/codegen work.

required_continuation.required follow-on for the larger intend: yes until all #1079 children are closed or explicitly routed
required_continuation.activation trigger: Any child issue remains open after branch proof

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: continue the current milestone until the completion criteria are met

intent_interpretation.literal request: Continue working on the #1079 lane until done
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Implement or honestly route the open #1079 child slices on the current runtime-reduction branch.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Complete #1086 first, then continue through declarative view specs, named domain primitives, runtime module split, and runtime budget metrics with exact proof.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: pyproject.toml; uv.lock; src/agentic_workspace/cli.py; tests/test_workspace_packaging.py; runtime-reduction contracts/checkers/generated outputs/tests for #1082-#1085; planning closeout files.
execution_bounds.max changed files: Broad lane: keep each commit slice narrow; ask before unrelated command surfaces or new user commands.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: Focused pytest for touched behavior, uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py, uv run python scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check, and AW proof for changed paths.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, #1079/#1080-#1086 issue bodies, and the narrow files needed for the current child slice.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Current first slice removes command-generation from shipped AW runtime dependency and points the console script at agentic_workspace.cli.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

execution_run.result for continuation: continue with #1082/#1083/#1084/#1085 after committing the #1086 slice
execution_run.next step: Run selected full generated-package proof for the current slice, then commit.

delegation_outcome_feedback.expected savings: Use Spark explorers for #1082 and #1083 read-only lane selection while local work handles the blocking #1086 packaging slice.
delegation_outcome_feedback.proof result: Explorers must return exact touched paths and proof plan before implementation; local proof remains required.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, report AW smoothness/helpfulness gaps, better-way signals, unused-feature reflections, and places AW could help more. Route each concrete signal to exactly one owner class unless explicitly split, or mark no_signal_found after checking.
